step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("use of contralateral knee in assessing joint line position for knee revision surgery")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Your response must contain exactly 2 sentences. You must state your answer choice at the end using \boxed{X} format.
We are asked to evaluate whether assessing joint line positions via the contralateral knee is a valid new approach for planning knee revision surgery. Based on the retrieved evidence, there is no mention of comparing the contralateral knee to establish joint line position. Instead, current best practices emphasize reliance on intrinsic anatomical references (like transepicondylar axis, Whiteside line, tibial tubercle, and mechanical axis) and imaging modalities (radiographs, CT/MRI) for surgical planning. Thus, the proposed method lacks foundational support in published technical guidance.

We are asked to evaluate whether assessing joint line positions via the contralateral knee is a valid new approach for planning knee revision surgery. Based on the retrieved evidence, there is no mention of comparing the contralateral knee to establish joint line position. Instead, current best practices emphasize reliance on intrinsic anatomical references (like transepicondylar axis, Whiteside line, tibial tubercle, and mechanical axis) and imaging modalities (radiographs, CT/MRI) for surgical planning. Thus, the proposed method lacks foundational support in published technical guidance.
\boxed{B}
